title: EAFIX Modular Services and Techspec Session Reference for AI Agents
purpose: Give an AI agent enough context about the terminal session, downloaded authoritative documents, services directory layout, service inventory, and generated services file tree without requiring access to the original terminal chat.

source_limitations: This reference is derived from terminal transcript output, not a fresh filesystem scan performed by this assistant.
The services file count is preserved as reported in the transcript: approximately 250 source files, excluding __pycache__ and .pyc files.
The transport-router portion of the transcript contained duplicated lines; normalized_paths removes duplicate path entries while services_tree_raw preserves the original transcript tree.
Techspec download verification command returned no output in the transcript after the download step, but the transcript explicitly states all 19 Markdown files downloaded successfully.
The transcript visually truncates the download command output; exact downloaded filenames should be verified in C:\Users\richg\eafix-modular\EAFIX_auth_docs.

techspec_folder.download_status: All 19 Markdown files reportedly downloaded successfully to local_authoritative_docs_path.
techspec_folder.filename_observation_note: The GitHub table in the transcript displays 0110000063-style filenames, while the download output shows at least the first three files with longer 0110000063260118-style numeric prefixes. Use an actual directory listing of EAFIX_auth_docs before relying on exact local filenames.

architecture_patterns.description: Hyphenated service directories such as data-ingestor contain implementation files; underscore directories such as data_ingestor act as stable import wrappers.
Implementation files carry numeric ID prefixes, for example 2099900112260118_main.py, for traceability.
.dir_id files are present for directory identity tracking.
Every service is expected to expose plugin.py inheriting from BasePlugin or equivalent shared plugin base.
Typical service structure includes config.py, health.py, metrics.py, main.py, and plugin.py, with FastAPI-style main entrypoints where applicable.

service_inventory.purpose: Normalizes MT4/DDE broker feeds into PriceTick events
Tkinter GUI, MQ4 scripts, indicator overlays
Shell scripts for enterprise service integration/validation
Event routing and validation between services

key_service_flow.flow_text: data-ingestor -> indicator-engine -> signal-generator -> execution-engine -> risk-manager -> reentry-engine <- reentry-matrix-svc -> transport-router -> telemetry-daemon; telemetry-daemon is cross-cutting.

how_to_use_this_reference: Use techspec_folder.listed_items to locate authoritative Markdown documents downloaded into EAFIX_auth_docs.
Use services_directory.service_inventory to understand service purposes before reading implementation files.
Use services_directory.architecture_patterns to avoid breaking naming, identity, plugin, or wrapper conventions.
Use services_directory.normalized_paths for machine-readable path lookup; use services_tree_raw if exact transcript fidelity is needed.
Treat this file as a session-derived reference, not as a substitute for reading current source files before making code changes.
development_warnings: Do not remove numeric ID prefixes without a migration plan.
Do not treat underscore wrapper directories as duplicate dead code; they provide stable import paths.
Do not ignore .dir_id files; they are part of the project identity scheme.
Do not create a custom service shape that bypasses config/health/metrics/main/plugin conventions unless explicitly justified.
Before implementing or modifying a service, cross-check the relevant techspec document and service inventory entry.
recommended_next_ai_actions: Generate or update a repository-level service registry from normalized_paths and service_inventory.
Validate each service has expected config/health/metrics/main/plugin files or document exceptions.
Map service files to module_catalog.json and process_step_catalog.json for traceability.
Create drift report comparing EAFIX_auth_docs techspec expectations to services implementation paths.
